title: Five takeaways from the first round of French local elections
subhead: The 2026 municipal elections have already concluded in the vast majority of French towns, with a mayor being directly elected in the first round. In larger cities, however, the right and the Socialists are in the lead for the second round.

title: 'This is not our war': Europe refuses to be dragged into Trump's war with Iran
subhead: The American president has demanded that his allies help unblock the Strait of Hormuz for Gulf oil tankers. France aims to lead an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation, but only once hostilities have ended.

title: 'For Trump, the Middle East war is a way to avenge the United States' past military traumas'
subhead: Political scientist and US specialist Marie-Cécile Naves analyzes the narratives coming from the White House, highlighting a sensationalist war communications strategy that blurs the lines between fiction, reality and references from popular culture.

title: Third trial begins for Chilean man accused of murdering ex-girlfriend in France
subhead: Twice sentenced to 28 years in prison, Nicolas Zepeda has always maintained his innocence. If convicted again, this time he will have to pay for a portion of the trial's costs.

title: Michelin Guide 2026: Michaël Arnoult, the only new three-star chef
subhead: The chef at Les Morainières earned a coveted third star in the latest edition of the Michelin Guide, which was unveiled in Monaco on Monday. It was a triumph for the reserved master chef.

French local elections: First-round results give sense of political landscape one year before presidential vote
subhead: With blurred lines between the right and far right, rivalry between the radical left and the Socialists and the fading of the presidential party, the period between the two rounds of the municipal vote is a testing ground for political power balances ahead of the 2027 election.

title: How Epstein made himself indispensable to MoMA's former chair Leon Black
subhead: Disclosed files reveal close ties between the late financier and the billionaire art market funder. Emails, testimonies and financial documents raise new questions about the businessman's involvement in Jeffrey Epstein's sex trafficking networks.

title: 'In 1979, the Iranian left played a major role in the rise of the Islamic Republic'
subhead: Philosopher Stéphanie Roza and political scientist Amirpasha Tavakkoli explain how, throughout the 20th century, the political turmoil in Iran, torn between modernity and anti-modernity, ultimately led to Islamist victory.
